behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies: this behavior depends on real git rename detection and CLI subprocess interaction with an actual repository. A fake git response could miss failures where command flags or parsing of real rename status output are wrong. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered, but behavior text requires true rename handling from repository history; that disqualifies stub-only module wiring. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI/LLM decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit a file, rename it with `git mv`, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert report shows 1 renamed, 0 added, 0 deleted for that change set.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE apply: binary diff behavior is produced by real git + real file content handling, and mocks can hide parser/encoding crashes seen only with real subprocess output. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered, but behavior explicitly references binary diffs in real repo context, which disqualifies in-process-only tests. No AI dependency, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, commit a binary file (e.g., random bytes), modify it, run the CLI subprocess, assert exit code is success and binary file is reflected in counts without traceback/crash.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies: unborn-branch/no-commit states are git process behaviors (often fatal HEAD errors) that must be exercised against a real repo and subprocess. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered, but behavior text is specifically about repository state with no commits, which is an external git state boundary. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified: no model output involved.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess, assert it exits successfully and prints an empty structured report (not an error).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is end-to-end against real git history; correctness depends on interaction between repository state, git command output, and report aggregation. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing history can mask mismatches caused by real git output forms across statuses. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered, but behavior explicitly requires matching actual repository history, disqualifying purely in-process fixtures. No AI steps, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a known sequence of commits containing adds/deletes/renames/modifies, run CLI subprocess for the target range, and assert reported category counts equal manually expected counts from that history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA applies: staged vs unstaged semantics are git index/worktree behaviors that require real repository state transitions and real git commands. Integration-level fakes can miss differences in how index/worktree deltas are emitted. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered, but behavior text explicitly compares staged and unstaged repository states, which are OS/git state boundaries. No LLM dependence, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, make equivalent edits in two runs (one staged via `git add`, one unstaged), run CLI subprocess in each state, and assert normalized structured outputs are identical.
